channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model for on-device function calling, enabling efficient AI tasks offline on mobile devices. This innovation enhances user experience by allowing complex actions like calendar management and game logic execution without internet dependency.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools, allowing developers to connect their AI agents to platforms like GitHub and Notion. This ecosystem aims to facilitate the creation of more capable and practical AI applications.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is set for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases AI's role in enhancing event experiences. This tradition aims to engage attendees and highlight the innovative use of AI in event planning.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring keynotes available online. The event will cover topics like Gemini multimodal breakthroughs and zero-trust security, emphasizing the importance of in-person collaboration.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers faster GPU and NPU acceleration and improved support for PyTorch and JAX. This update positions LiteRT as a key framework for deploying advanced AI and on-device inference.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through interactive, hands-on building. The program covers advanced topics and provides immediate, practical results for participants.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has introduced updates to improve coding workflows, including features like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These tools aim to make AI a more tailored collaborator for developers, enhancing productivity.

item.title: Copilot is ‘for entertainment purposes only,’ according to Microsoft’s terms of use
item.description: Microsoft's terms of use for Copilot emphasize that it is 'for entertainment purposes only,' cautioning users against blindly trusting AI outputs. This highlights ongoing concerns about AI reliability and the need for critical evaluation.

item.title: Can orbital data centers help justify a massive valuation for SpaceX?
item.description: The latest episode of TechCrunch’s Equity podcast discusses Elon Musk's vision for orbital data centers and their potential impact on SpaceX's valuation. The conversation explores the feasibility and implications of such ambitious projects.

item.title: In Japan, the robot isn’t coming for your job; it’s filling the one nobody wants
item.description: In Japan, the deployment of physical AI is being accelerated to address labor shortages, focusing on roles that are less desirable. This shift reflects a growing trend of integrating AI into the workforce to fill gaps.

item.title: Suno is a music copyright nightmare
item.description: Suno, an AI music platform, claims to prevent the use of copyrighted material, but its system has flaws, allowing unauthorized content to slip through. This raises concerns about copyright infringement in AI-generated music.

item.title: I let Gemini in Google Maps plan my day and it went surprisingly well
item.description: Using Gemini in Google Maps, users can plan their day more effectively, showcasing the AI's integration into everyday tools. The positive experience indicates potential for AI to enhance user convenience.

item.title: Grammarly’s sloppelganger saga
item.description: Grammarly's journey reveals the complexities of AI development, highlighting both successes and challenges faced by the company. This saga reflects broader trends in AI's integration into writing tools.

item.title: dhisana 0.0.1.dev305
item.description: Dhisana AI Platform has released a new Python SDK, enabling developers to integrate its capabilities into their applications. This enhances accessibility for developers interested in AI solutions.

item.title: agentsec-gateway added to PyPI
item.description: A new security gateway for MCP server connections has been added to PyPI, enhancing security protocols for developers using this model. This addition supports safer AI integrations.

item.title: AI-Driven school expanding to major US cities despite union pushback
item.description: Alpha Schools is expanding its AI-driven education model across the U.S. despite facing pushback from unions and concerns over its untested approach. This move could reshape educational practices.

item.title: tachikoma-agent 1.3.0
item.description: The tachikoma-agent 1.3.0 has been released, offering a proactive personal assistant built on the Claude Agent SDK. This development enhances personal assistant capabilities in AI.

item.title: langchain-signalfuse 0.1.2
item.description: LangChain has introduced tools for the SignalFuse crypto trading signal API, providing developers with new resources for integrating AI in trading applications. This could enhance trading strategies.

item.title: mcp-clipboard added to PyPI
item.description: The mcp-clipboard has been added to PyPI, allowing MCP servers to interact with system clipboards, facilitating data handling across formats. This tool enhances usability for developers.

item.title: linafish added to PyPI
item.description: Linafish has been introduced, a tool that analyzes writing to reveal thought patterns. This could provide insights into cognitive processes through AI analysis.

item.title: graqle 0.42.5
item.description: Graqle 0.42.5 introduces architecture-aware reasoning for AI tools, allowing users to build knowledge graphs from codebases. This enhances dependency and impact analysis with confidence scores.

item.title: mindvault added to PyPI
item.description: Mindvault has been added to PyPI, offering a local-first second brain system that enables users to interact with their own data. This tool focuses on retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).

item.title: Show HN: Nelson, a Ralph-like loop for finding vulnerabilities
item.description: Nelson, a tool for finding vulnerabilities, has been showcased on HN. It operates similarly to Ralph, providing developers with a means to identify security flaws.

item.title: promptwall added to PyPI
item.description: Promptwall has been added to PyPI, featuring a firewall for LLM prompt injections. It includes session tracking, explainability, and multilingual detection capabilities.

item.title: langchain-signalfuse 0.1.1
item.description: Langchain-signalfuse 0.1.1 provides tools for integrating with the SignalFuse crypto trading signal API, enhancing trading strategies with AI.

item.title: cane-ai added to PyPI
item.description: Cane-ai has been added to PyPI, offering open-source infrastructure for building, evaluating, fine-tuning, and deploying AI agents. This tool aims to streamline AI agent development.

item.title: Fact Check: New York Times DID Publish Headline Saying 'A North American Treaty Organization Without America?' -- Should Be Atlantic, Not American
item.description: A fact check confirmed that The New York Times published a headline mistakenly referring to a 'North American Treaty Organization' instead of 'Atlantic'.

item.title: Fact Check: New York Times DID Publish Headline Saying 'A North American Treaty Organization Without America?' -- Should Be Atlantic, Not American
item.description: This article repeats the fact check regarding The New York Times' headline error, confirming the mistake in the April 3, 2026 edition.

item.title: Show HN: Harness CLI – Open-source agent loop for long-running app development
item.description: Harness CLI, an open-source tool for long-running app development, has been introduced on HN, providing a framework for managing application lifecycles.

item.title: bernstein 1.4.14
item.description: Bernstein 1.4.14 offers declarative agent orchestration aimed at engineering teams, enhancing collaboration and project management in AI development.

item.title: langchain-signalfuse added to PyPI
item.description: LangChain has added tools for the SignalFuse crypto trading signal API to PyPI, enhancing accessibility for developers. This integration allows for improved trading signal processing within the LangChain framework.

item.title: langchain-signalfuse 0.1.0
item.description: The release of LangChain tools version 0.1.0 for the SignalFuse crypto trading signal API offers new functionalities for developers. This update is part of LangChain's ongoing efforts to support crypto trading applications.

item.title: langchain-modexia 0.1.0
item.description: LangChain has launched version 0.1.0 of its tools for the Modexia AgentPay ecosystem, providing official integrations. This development aims to streamline payment processes within the ecosystem.

item.title: fused-local-corr 0.3.29
item.description: The release of Fused Local Correlation version 0.3.29 in PyTorch introduces updates for improved performance in machine learning tasks. This version continues to enhance the functionality of the Fused Local Correlation library.

item.title: fused-local-corr 0.3.28
item.description: Fused Local Correlation version 0.3.28 has been released in PyTorch, contributing to ongoing improvements in the library's capabilities. This update is part of a series of enhancements to the Fused Local Correlation tool.

item.title: fused-local-corr 0.3.27
item.description: Fused Local Correlation version 0.3.27 has been released, adding to the ongoing updates in the PyTorch library. This version aims to further optimize local correlation computations.

item.title: fused-local-corr 0.3.211
item.description: The release of Fused Local Correlation version 0.3.211 in PyTorch continues to enhance the library's functionality. This version is part of a series of incremental updates aimed at improving performance.

item.title: fused-local-corr 0.3.210
item.description: Fused Local Correlation version 0.3.210 has been released in PyTorch, contributing to the library's ongoing development. This version is among several recent updates aimed at improving efficiency.

item.title: AI firm leader robbed at knifepoint, says attackers sought crypto
item.description: An AI firm leader was robbed at knifepoint, with attackers reportedly seeking cryptocurrency. This incident underscores the growing security concerns in the crypto industry, highlighting the need for better protective measures.

item.title: How many products does Microsoft have named 'Copilot'? I mapped every one
item.description: An analysis maps out the various products named 'Copilot' from Microsoft, revealing the company's extensive use of the brand across different applications. This exploration sheds light on Microsoft's strategy in AI tools.

item.title: GPT-5.4 Pro jumps to 150 IQ on MESNA Norway test as OpenAI breaks its own record
item.description: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Pro model achieved an IQ score of 150, surpassing 99.96% of humans, indicating significant advancements in AI capabilities. This milestone suggests that AI development is accelerating beyond typical product cycles.

item.title: Sony Acquires AI Company Cinemersive Labs
item.description: Sony has acquired AI startup Cinemersive Labs, enhancing its focus on machine learning-driven graphics. This acquisition reflects the growing importance of AI in consumer technology.

item.title: How to opt out of AI data collection in popular apps
item.description: Users can learn how to opt out of AI data collection in popular apps like ChatGPT, Google, Alexa, and Siri. This guide addresses privacy concerns regarding AI data usage.

item.title: The Multilingual Gift
item.description: A neuroscientist's experiment with AI across four languages reveals unique cognitive aspects of multilingual individuals that AI cannot replicate. This study emphasizes the value of human language processing.

item.title: idliappam added to PyPI
item.description: The Python SDK for AI Commerce OS has been added to PyPI, enabling integration with WhatsApp, invoicing, inventory, and loyalty systems. This tool enhances AI capabilities in commerce.

item.title: My 5 biggest takeaways after using the Motorola Razr Ultra 2025 for a year
item.description: After a year of using the Motorola Razr Ultra 2025, the author shares key insights about the phone's performance and features. The review reflects on user experience and product evolution.

item.title: Apple’s latest acquisition is telling sign of where the company is headed
item.description: Apple's acquisition of MotionVFX, a Final Cut Pro plugin maker, suggests potential enhancements for its Creator Studio. This move indicates Apple's strategic direction towards content creation tools.

item.title: Anthropic says Claude Code subscribers will need to pay extra for OpenClaw usage
item.description: Claude Code subscribers will face additional charges for using Anthropic's OpenClaw and other third-party tools. This change may affect user adoption and pricing strategies.

item.title: mcp-ezpay-einvoice 0.1.0
item.description: The MCP server has introduced ezPay E-Invoice API tools for Taiwan, enhancing AI agent capabilities in e-invoicing operations. This development supports digital transaction efficiency.

item.title: Samsung The Frame Pro TV (2026): Everything You Need To Know
item.description: Samsung's The Frame Pro TV (2026) was announced at CES 2026, featuring improved picture quality and new software capabilities. This model continues the evolution of lifestyle TVs.
